report_name: Iranian Hackers Launch Coordinated Password Spray Attacks on Middle East

threat_actors.name: Gray Sandstorm
threat_actors.description: An Iranian Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) group suspected of conducting a large-scale password spray campaign against government and private sector organizations in Israel and the UAE. The attacks appear to be coordinated with physical military operations for intelligence gathering and Bombing Damage Assessment (BDA).
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering and Bombing Damage Assessment (BDA) to support kinetic military operations.

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force: Password Spraying
attack_patterns.description: Used as the primary initial access vector, attempting a small number of common passwords against a large number of Microsoft 365 user accounts to avoid account lockouts.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: After successfully guessing a password, the attackers used the compromised credentials to gain legitimate access to the victim's Microsoft 365 cloud account.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Identity Information: Email Addresses
attack_patterns.description: The attackers gathered lists of valid email addresses for the target organizations to conduct the password spray attack.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access to Microsoft 365 accounts, the attackers collected data from cloud storage sources like Exchange Online (emails) and SharePoint Online (documents).

identities.name: Government and private sector organizations in Israel and the UAE
identities.description: A class of organizations in Israel and the UAE targeted by Gray Sandstorm's password spray campaign starting in early March 2026.

identities.name: Municipalities in Israel and the UAE
identities.description: Municipalities responsible for civil defense and damage response were specifically targeted to gather intelligence supporting kinetic military strikes.

indicators.name: Microsoft 365 Password Spray Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Microsoft 365 Failed Logon Attempt
id: 2b9a8c1d-6f3e-4b1a-9c8d-7e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects a failed logon attempt to Microsoft 365 (Entra ID), indicated by ResultType 50126. This event is a building block for detecting password spray attacks when correlated in a SIEM to identify many failures from a single IP across multiple accounts.
references:
  - https://risky.biz/bulletin-apr1-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1110.003
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate users entering incorrect passwords.
level: informational
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    ResultType: '50126'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a failed logon attempt to Microsoft 365 (Entra ID). This is a key event for building higher-fidelity password spray detections, which typically involve correlating a high volume of these failures from a single source IP across many different user accounts.

relationships.target_name: Gather Victim Identity Information: Email Addresses
Government and private sector organizations in Israel and the UAE
Municipalities in Israel and the UAE
